channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: What to know about Hurricane Milton after it slammed Florida's Gulf Coast
item.description: As Hurricane Milton barreled into and across Florida, it spawned dozens of tornadoes, knocked out power to millions, damaged homes, flooded neighborhoods and led to hundreds of rescues. But the damage wasn't as bad as had been feared. Gov.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Massive hole swallows 2 homes in Florida
item.description: The wild scene raised concerns about whether the ground collapse was caused by a sinkhole or water damage from sewage issues.
item.dc:creator: Austin Kellerman, Fox 5

item.title: RFK Jr. reacts to his ‘fearless’ mother Ethel’s passing: ‘Invented tough love’
item.description: Robert F. Kennedy Jr. took to social media on Thursday to commemorate the life of his late mother, Ethel Kennedy, in a heartfelt post.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden suggests Trump 'get a life' after false hurricane claims
item.description: “Mr. President Trump, former President Trump, get a life man, help these people,” Biden said in remarks on Hurricane Milton from the White House.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Hurricane Milton disrupts Yom Kippur plans for Jews in Florida
item.description: WINTER PARK, Florida (AP) — Many Jews worldwide will mark Yom Kippur in fasting and prayer at their synagogues this weekend.
item.dc:creator: MARIAM FAM, GIOVANNA DELL'ORTO and HOLLY MEYER, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Viral Tampa sensation 'Lieutenant Dan,' who rode out Hurricane Milton in boat, describes how he survived
item.description: A Florida man named Joseph Malinowski, referred to by Tampa locals as "Lt. Dan," went viral on TikTok before his criminal background was revealed to users.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Condemned inmate Richard Moore wants someone other than South Carolina's governor to decide clemency
item.description: COLUMBIA, S.C. (AP) — A South Carolina inmate scheduled to be executed in just over three weeks is asking a federal judge to take away the power of granting clemency from the governor who is a former state attorney general and place it with a parole board.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Tampa woman killed by falling branch after Hurricane Milton
item.description: Investigators said that it appears that reconstruction efforts were being done in the area when the branch fell on the victim. So far, the death appears to be an accident.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, NewsNation

item.title: Photos: First look at Siesta Key after Hurricane Milton
item.description: Aerial images show the damage in Siesta Key, and in Osprey, which is located just inland of the island.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Klynstra, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Milton spawned high number of destructive, deadly tornadoes
item.description: FT. PIERCE, Fla. (AP) — Dozens of tornadoes spawned by Hurricane Milton caught many Floridians by surprise as they braced for heavy rain, strong winds and storm surges. Violent twisters were seen crossing highways, ripping off roofs and downing trees and power lines.
item.dc:creator: STEPHANY MATAT, ISABELLA O'MALLEY and SUMAN NAISHADHAM, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Interim NYPD Commissioner Tom Donlon could resign: sources
item.description: NEW YORK (PIX11) – Less than a month after the NYPD's interim commissioner was appointed, another major shake-up could be coming. Interim NYPD Commissioner Tom Donlon is expected to step down, sources said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Finn Hoogensen, NewsNation

item.title: Hurricane Milton’s winds topple crane that was building west Florida’s tallest residential tower
item.description: The mayor in St. Petersburg, Florida, warned residents that cranes at several construction projects across the city might fall in Hurricane Milton and at the storm's peak on Wednesday night, one came crashing down.
item.dc:creator: JEFFREY COLLINS, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Indiana growers seeing above average pumpkin crops this fall
item.description: "These are some of the most beautiful pumpkins we've raised in years," said Kyle Spencer, co-owner of Spencer Farm in Noblesville.
item.dc:creator: Scarlett O’Hara, NewsNation

item.title: Milton by the numbers: At least 5 dead, at least 12 tornadoes, 3.4M without power
item.description: Hurricane Milton's explosive formation stirred high anxiety for Florida less than two weeks after Helene raked the state and much of the Southeast. On Thursday, authorities were still assessing the damage, even as Gov. Ron DeSantis said it fell short of “the worst-case scenario.
item.dc:creator: MARY KATHERINE WILDEMAN and CHRISTOPHER L. KELLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Rare comet visible in the night sky won't return for 80,000 years
item.description: A rare comet is expected to be visible to the naked eye across the Northern Hemisphere for several weeks in October.
item.dc:creator: Sergio Robles, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Milton and Helene leave students, schools reeling
item.description: As Hurricane Milton slams into Florida — and amid Helene's lingering destruction — students across the southeast are facing severe disruptions to their academic year, posing challenges for both educators and children after they have already witnessed devastation in their communities.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: 14-year-old found floating on fence in Tampa floodwaters after Hurricane Milton
item.description: A 14-year-old had to be rescued after Hurricane Milton's floodwaters trapped him on a floating fence in the middle of Tampa.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Ethel Kennedy, social activist and widow of Robert F. Kennedy, dies at 96
item.description: Ethel Kennedy, the wife of Sen. Robert F. Kennedy who raised their 11 children after he was assassinated and remained dedicated to social causes and the family’s legacy for decades thereafter, died on Thursday, her family said. She was 96.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL CASEY and STEVE LeBLANC, Associated Press, KTLA 5

item.title: Bacon hogs the spotlight in election debates, but reasons for its sizzling inflation are complex
item.description: She blames greedy companies for price-gouging. He blames the Biden administration’s economic policies. Kamala Harris and Donald Trump agree on one thing: Tapping into sour consumer sentiment about high grocery prices is one way to court voters. Bacon prices have been a particular focus for Trump.
item.dc:creator: DEE-ANN DURBIN, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: CNN's Anderson Cooper hit by flying object during Milton live shot
item.description: CNN’s Anderson Cooper was hit by a flying object on Wednesday during a live shot while reporting from Florida on Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Arizona college student accused in random classmate stabbing has attempted murder charge dropped
item.description: ASU stabbing suspect Kaci Sloan has entered a not-guilty plea. Sloan is charged with stabbing a fellow student multiple times in a classroom on Sept. 19.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: Milton causes widespread power outages and several deaths in Florida
item.description: Hurricane Milton brought powerful winds, storm surge and flooding to much of Florida after making landfall along the Gulf Coast as a Category 3 storm.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Video: Wind tears roof off Tropicana Field, home of the Rays
item.description: Hurricane Milton made landfall in Siesta Key south of St. Petersburg, and while the city avoided some of the worst storm surges that doesn't mean the city avoided major effects from the storm.
item.dc:creator: Garrett Phillips, Fox 5

item.title: Lighting and furniture store to pay $4.1M in 'deceptive' pricing lawsuit
item.description: A lighting and furniture company based in California will pay more than $4 million to settle a civil lawsuit that accused the corporation of deceptive pricing and false advertising. Lamps Plus, Inc.
item.dc:creator: Travis Schlepp, Fox 5

item.title: Hurricane Milton makes landfall, slamming into Florida with destructive winds, catastrophic storm surge
item.description: More than 3 million people in Florida are without power Thursday morning as destructive Hurricane Milton is making its way across the state.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: NASA video shows Hurricane Milton from space
item.description: Video taken from the International Space Station puts the massive size of Hurricane Milton into perspective.
item.dc:creator: Alix Martichoux, Fox 5

item.title: Tropical dictionary: Words to know as active hurricane season continues
item.description: Let’s dive into some of the frequently used words and phrases that are on repeat during this busy week of hurricane season.
item.dc:creator: Alex Schneider, Fox 5

item.title: COVID may increase risk of heart attack, death years after infection: study
item.description: COVID-19 could increase the risk of major cardiac events, like strokes and heart attacks, or death from any cause, according to a new study.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Tampa's 'Lt. Dan' says he's riding out Hurricane Milton on boat despite mayor's warning
item.description: A Tampa man who goes by the nickname "Lt. Dan" has caused a flurry of online concern for his safety during Hurricane Milton.
item.dc:creator: Nathaniel Rodriguez, Fox 5

item.title: Jenna Fischer of 'The Office' reveals breast cancer diagnosis, recovery
item.description: Jenna Fischer, the actress well-known for her role as Pam on "The Office," revealed her diagnosis and recovery in a post to social media.
item.dc:creator: Joey Schneider, Fox 5

item.title: Can Waffle House really help predict a storm's severity?
item.description: When hurricanes tear through Southeastern towns, the hot meals and bold yellow signs of the local Waffle House provide another kind of comfort.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Helene spurs IV fluid shortage. Milton could make it worse
item.description: Hospitals across the U.S. are feeling the impact of Hurricane Helene as experts warn of an imminent intravenous (IV) solution shortage due to the temporary shutdown of a facility in North Carolina.
item.dc:creator: Joseph Choi, Fox 5

item.title: Why do people fill their bathtubs with water before a hurricane?
item.description: If you live in a hurricane-prone area, it may be common practice for you. If you live well outside that region, you may be confused by the move.
item.dc:creator: Addy Bink, ABC 4

item.title: iPhone update allows users to text for help when there's no cell service. How it works
item.description: With Hurricane Milton barreling toward Florida's Gulf Coast, many Tampa Bay area residents are fearing widespread and potentially weeks-long power outages, which could affect cellphone service, as many saw during Hurricane Helene.
item.dc:creator: Kaycee Sloan, ABC 4

item.title: Florida has nearly all ballots counted on Election Day, while California can take weeks. This is why
item.description: Why is it that some states have nearly all the ballots counted within hours of polls closing while others take weeks?
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: Where to donate blood to help American Red Cross, Helene & Milton victims
item.description: The American Red Cross is in urgent need of blood donations due to the devastation caused by Hurricane Helene and the lead-up to Hurricane Milton, and donors can make an appointment by visiting RedCrossBlood.org, calling 1-800-RED CROSS, or using the Red Cross Blood Donor App.
item.dc:creator: Eli Ong, WGN 9

item.title: What polling shows about Black voters' views of Harris and Trump
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — Black registered voters have an overwhelmingly positive view of Vice President Kamala Harris, but they’re less sure that she would change the country for the better, according to a recent poll from the AP-NORC Center for Public Affairs Research.
item.dc:creator: MATT BROWN and LINLEY SANDERS, Associated Press, ABC 4
